name: Pan-Roasted Halibut with Chermoula
description: Meaty halibut fillets pan-roasted and finished with North African chermoula (cilantro-garlic-cumin-paprika herb sauce). Pair with the spiced vegetable couscous.
recipeIngredient: 2 skin-on halibut steaks (1.5 lb each), 1 to 1.5 inches thick, trimmed
recipeInstructions: For the chermoula: Process cilantro, oil, lemon juice, garlic, cumin, paprika, salt, and cayenne in a food processor until smooth, about 1 minute, scraping down the sides as needed. Set aside for serving.

For the fish: Adjust oven rack to middle position and heat oven to 325°F. Pat halibut dry and season with salt and pepper. Heat oil in a 12-inch ovensafe nonstick skillet over medium-high heat until just smoking. Add halibut and cook until well browned, about 5 minutes.
Gently flip the halibut using two spatulas, then transfer the skillet to the oven. Roast until the halibut flakes apart when prodded with a paring knife and registers 140°F, 6 to 9 minutes.

Carefully transfer halibut to a cutting board, tent loosely with foil, and rest 5 minutes. Remove the skin and separate each quadrant of meat from the bones by slipping a knife between them. Serve with chermoula.
author: America's Test Kitchen

ai.cliffNotes: Meaty halibut fillets pan-roasted and finished with North African chermoula (cilantro-garlic-cumin-paprika herb sauce). Pair with the spiced vegetable couscous.
keyTips: Halibut steaks are big and delicate — flip them with two spatulas so they do not break apart.
Pull the fish at 140°F for moist, just-cooked meat; overcooking is the main risk with lean halibut.
A 12-inch ovensafe nonstick skillet is essential so the same pan goes from stovetop sear to oven roast.
Swordfish (1 to 1.5 inches thick) can be substituted for the halibut; adjust the oven time as needed.
Chermoula is a bright Moroccan herb sauce — make it first so it is ready the moment the fish comes out.
